description: A hand slides across a table while the person beside it remains in the same room. That local change is 'roke', as is a body's larger relocation. 'wepu' (go) follows a subject along a path; 'thalo' (walk) names walking as one way to move.
articulatory_notes: Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge, then round the lips for stressed middle-height back 'o'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k', release it, and move the tongue forward while unrounding the lips for middle-height front 'e'.
usage_notes: Without causative 'ka', the moving person or thing is the subject; with it, an agent moves someone or something else. Prepositional phrases place or orient the motion.

examples.phi: tenoa mua mia manuwe reshi roke.
examples.translation: The tool moves quickly in my hand.

semantic_domains.activity: changing position or continuing in motion
semantic_domains.physical: motion of a body or material object
